task_objective: Remove CPU-wasting timeout polling from main.py::_run_blocking while preserving Decky backend cancellation and worker-thread completion behavior.
tests_added: Updated tests/test_main.py::test_run_blocking_awaits_threadsafe_future_without_polling to forbid asyncio.wait_for and TimeoutError polling in _run_blocking.
design_decisions: Kept the existing dedicated worker thread, contextvars.copy_context(), asyncio.Future, and loop.call_soon_threadsafe() bridge.
Replaced the 50ms wait_for timeout loop with a direct await of asyncio.shield(future).
Did not switch to asyncio.to_thread() or run_in_executor because prior hardening and current sandbox checks showed those routes have the same thread-wakeup sensitivity in this environment.
Used official Decky template/import documentation, current plugin examples, and Python asyncio documentation to ground the plan.
results: Confirmed the updated regression failed before implementation because main.py still referenced wait_for.

Focused _run_blocking and cancellation tests passed outside the sandbox; the sandboxed selector did not wake on loop.call_soon_threadsafe without a timer, so sandboxed async/thread tests were not representative.
